decisions.question: How should polycystic ovarian morphology (PCOM) be confirmed in adults?

optionsConsidered.label: Serum AMH (alternative to ultrasound in adults)
optionsConsidered.sourceQuote: Serum AMH can be used as an alternative to ultrasound for the detection of PCOM in adults.

optionsConsidered.label: Pelvic ultrasound (Rotterdam)
optionsConsidered.sourceQuote: PCOM defined as ≥12 follicles per ovary or ovarian volume ≥10 mL on ultrasound.

decisions.chosen: Serum AMH (alternative to ultrasound in adults)

decisions.question: What is first-line pharmacological ovulation induction for anovulatory infertility?

optionsConsidered.label: Letrozole
optionsConsidered.sourceQuote: Letrozole resulted in higher live-birth and ovulation rates than clomiphene among women with PCOS.

optionsConsidered.label: Clomiphene citrate
optionsConsidered.sourceQuote: Clomiphene citrate has historically been the first-line agent for ovulation induction.

decisions.question: Which test for baseline glycaemic assessment?

optionsConsidered.label: 75 g 2-hour OGTT for BMI ≥ 25
optionsConsidered.sourceQuote: An OGTT is the preferred test to assess glycaemic status, especially where BMI ≥ 25 kg/m².

optionsConsidered.label: HbA1c or fasting glucose
optionsConsidered.sourceQuote: HbA1c offers a convenient single non-fasting sample for dysglycaemia screening.

decisions.question: Routine follow-up cadence for PCOS in this service line?

optionsConsidered.label: 6 months
optionsConsidered.sourceQuote: Stable patients may extend to six-monthly review.

optionsConsidered.label: Nurse-led care coordinator
optionsConsidered.sourceQuote: Follow-up is coordinated by the nurse-led PCOS clinic.

units.rationale: A confirmed PCOS diagnosis in a reproductive-age adult woman defines the cohort this protocol governs.

sourceRef.quote: PCOS is diagnosed in adults using the Rotterdam criteria (two of three of ovulatory dysfunction, hyperandrogenism, and PCOM).

units.rationale: BMI anchors metabolic risk stratification and the BMI-dependent screening thresholds below.

sourceRef.quote: BMI should be measured in all individuals with PCOS at diagnosis and during follow-up.

units.rationale: Blood pressure surveillance for the elevated cardiometabolic risk associated with PCOS.

sourceRef.quote: Blood pressure should be measured annually given increased cardiovascular risk.

units.rationale: Standardized hyperandrogenism assessment to guide cosmetic and pharmacological therapy.

sourceRef.quote: Use a standardized visual scale (e.g. modified Ferriman–Gallwey) to assess hirsutism.

units.content: Document menstrual cycle history & ovulatory status
units.rationale: Cycle pattern drives both the metabolic and the reproductive arms of the pathway.

sourceRef.quote: Cycle length and ovulatory status should be characterized to guide management.

units.rationale: The guideline mandates routine psychological screening; mood disorders are markedly more prevalent in PCOS.

sourceRef.quote: Screen all individuals with PCOS for depressive and anxiety symptoms at diagnosis.

units.content: Discuss lifestyle & weight management as first-line therapy
units.rationale: Lifestyle intervention is first-line for metabolic and reproductive outcomes when BMI ≥ 25.

sourceRef.quote: Lifestyle intervention is recommended as first-line management for weight and metabolic features.

units.content: Review metformin as an adjunct for metabolic features
units.rationale: Metformin may be added to lifestyle for weight and metabolic outcomes, particularly when BMI ≥ 25.

sourceRef.quote: Metformin should be considered in adults with PCOS and BMI ≥ 25 for weight and metabolic outcomes.

units.content: Discuss combined oral contraceptive pill for cycle regulation / hyperandrogenism
units.rationale: COCP is first-line for menstrual irregularity and hyperandrogenism in patients not currently pursuing pregnancy.

sourceRef.quote: Combined oral contraceptives are first-line for management of irregular cycles and clinical hyperandrogenism.

units.content: Order 75 g 2-hour oral glucose tolerance test (OGTT)
units.rationale: This org screens glycaemic status with a 75 g OGTT (not HbA1c) when BMI ≥ 25 and no result is on file.

sourceRef.quote: An OGTT is the preferred test to assess glycaemic status, especially where BMI ≥ 25 kg/m².

units.rationale: Baseline cardiovascular risk assessment when no recent lipid panel is on file.

sourceRef.quote: A fasting lipid profile should be measured at diagnosis and repeated based on risk.

units.content: Order serum AMH to define PCOM (in lieu of pelvic ultrasound)
units.rationale: Per the org decision, AMH is the adult PCOM marker instead of pelvic ultrasound when none is on file.

sourceRef.quote: Serum AMH can be used as an alternative to ultrasound for the detection of PCOM in adults.

units.content: Offer letrozole as first-line ovulation induction
units.rationale: For patients pursuing pregnancy, letrozole is first-line over clomiphene (higher live-birth rate).

sourceRef.quote: Letrozole was associated with higher live-birth and ovulation rates than clomiphene among infertile women with PCOS.

units.rationale: This org reviews active-management PCOS patients quarterly through the nurse-led clinic.

sourceRef.quote: Active-management patients are reviewed quarterly via the nurse-led PCOS clinic.

units.content: Summarize the basis for the PCOS diagnosis: ovulatory dysfunction, clinical/biochemical hyperandrogenism, and PCOM (by AMH).
units.rationale: Documents how the Rotterdam criteria were met for this patient.

sourceRef.quote: Document the criteria used to establish the diagnosis.

units.content: Document BMI, blood pressure, glycaemic and lipid status, and any screening ordered.
units.rationale: Captures the cardiometabolic assessment that anchors PCOS management.

sourceRef.quote: Assess and document cardiometabolic risk factors.

units.content: Document fertility intentions and the ovulation-induction or contraception plan.
units.rationale: Captures the reproductive arm of the plan and the decision made with the patient.

sourceRef.quote: Discuss and document reproductive goals and the agreed plan.

units.content: Document the result of mood/anxiety screening and any onward referral.
units.rationale: Routine psychological screening is mandated; this section makes its presence (or absence) explicit.

sourceRef.quote: Screen for and document psychological wellbeing.
